text: Train Claude on Mike Israetel's RP content for hypertrophy programming

Open questions for later: how to pull transcripts at scale, retrieval (RAG) vs fine-tuning, copyright/usage of the transcripts, and how to structure the program output.

text: Rome TD: clarify intro instructions (prospects → towers → combo towers, and 'tile' vs 'map')
notes: Player feedback: people are confused about prospects, how they become towers, and how towers become combo towers, and by the term 'tile' (maybe call it 'map'). Reword the start-of-game instructions to be clearer.
description: Rome TD onboarding feedback from players. Confusion points: (1) what a 'prospect' is, (2) how a prospect becomes a tower, (3) how a tower becomes a combo tower, (4) how to place and save prospects so they turn into towers. Action: rework the beginning-of-game instructions to explain prospects, their placement, and the prospect to tower to combo-tower progression more clearly, efficiently, and effectively. Separately, players are confused by the term 'tile' — consider renaming it to 'map', or clarifying what a tile is.

text: Build a daily to-do / daily task system for JB OS
notes: Think through a recurring "daily tasks" feature — model it on the Musubi daily designer Claude built
description: Goal: bring a daily-rhythm layer into JB OS. Claude already built a Musubi daily activity system (the musubi-designer that auto-generates ~15 Instagram-ready concepts every morning on a schedule). Apply that same 'daily generated batch + scheduled trigger' pattern to a personal daily to-do system. Things to think through: (1) a 'Today' view that surfaces what matters each day; (2) recurring/daily tasks that auto-regenerate; (3) carry-over of unfinished items to the next day; (4) a morning trigger that assembles the day's agenda; (5) how Musubi daily activities feed in as their own daily track. Come back to this — it's a someday/maybe idea, not urgent.
